2526.0Bloodstained Daggerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bloodstained_dagger_dagger_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.0Dagger with a bloodstained blade. Afflicts targets with blood loss. As blood darkened the dagger through repeated slashing and stabbing, its blade only grew sharper and harder.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 12}Slash/PierceDaggers(38) HemorrhageQuickstep3nannannannannannannannannannannan
2627.0Erdsteel Daggerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/erdsteel_dagger_dagger_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.0An erdsteel dagger with a grass crest engraved upon its blade. 2728.0Wakizashihttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/wakizashi_dagger_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.0Short sword with a tempering pattern on the blade. A weapon unique to warriors of the Land of Reeds. Afflicts targets with blood loss. These swords are considered auxiliary weapons. Yet, one man is known to wield this weapon in his left hand while using a full-sized katana in his right. A demonic swordsman known only as Okina.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 13}Slash/PierceDaggers(38) HemorrhageQuickstep3nannannannannannannannannannannan

5455.0Sword of St Trinahttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sword_of_st_trina_straight_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.0Silver sword carried by clerics of St. Trina. 5657.0Coded Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/coded_sword_straight_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.5Hidden sword once granted to the Tarnished of the Roundtable by the Two Fingers. Inflicts eternal sleep. When St. Trina was abandoned, the faint, light-purple mists coalesced into an intoxicating deep-purple cloud.1{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 12, 'Int': 14}Standard/PierceStraight Swords(42) SleepMists of Eternal Sleep23nannannannannannannannannannannan
5960.0Stone-Sheathed Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/stone-sheathed_sword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0A sword that none can pull from its scabbard of cracked stone. Relic of a lost civilization from a time long past, said to have served a ritual purpose at altars of light and darkness.1{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 8}StrikeStraight SwordsNo passive effectsSquare Off0nannannannannannannannannannannan
6061.0Sword of Lighthttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sword_of_light_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0Sword of light, pulled from its stone scabbard at an altar. From the quick of the root, unswerving rays of light intersect and reflect to give the silver blade form.1{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 11, 'Fai': 24}Standard/PierceStraight SwordsNo passive effectsLight30nannannannannannannannannannannan
6162.0Sword of Darknesshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sword_of_darkness_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0Sword of darkness, pulled from its stone scabbard at an altar. From the quick of the root, wandering coils of darkness coalesce and release, their eddies and vortices giving form to the dark blade.1{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 11, 'Fai': 24}Standard/PierceStraight SwordsNo passive effectsDarkness30nannannannannannannannannannannan
6263.0Bastard Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bastard_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0A large sword with a straight blade.This heavy sword is normally wielded with two hands, but those with sufficient strength can wield it in one alone.The hefty blade can be swung into multiple foes at once thanks to the broad, sweeping attack, making fending off crowds all the easier.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 10}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5 ( - 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
6364.0Claymorehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/claymore_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0A large sword with a long, straight blade. This heavy sword is usually wielded with two hands, but for those with enough strength, one is sufficient. In addition to the heavy sweeping attacks, it can also make a heavy piercing thrust making this a versatile weapon.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 13}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsLion's Claw20nannannannannannannannannannannan
6465.0Iron Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/iron_greatsword_greatswords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.0Greatsword with a blade of unusually thick steel. A coarse weapon to which polishing is a foreign concept. Weighty even among others of its class, the impact of each strike is sure to be felt.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 10}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)20nannannannannannannannannannannan
6566.0Lordsworn's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/lordsworns_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0Well-crafted straight sword with an illustrious design, wielded by regulars of a lord's army. Through blackened and damaged by years of use, it appears to have otherwise been kept in a serviceable condition, despite the soldiers having long since lost their minds.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 10}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5 (- 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
6667.0Knight's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/knights_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Greatsword bestowed upon skilled swordsmen in the demigods' service, and yet had no rank or title. Featuring a long grip enabling a dual-handed use, this weapon was designed to dispatch larger foes. Being charged with such a duty was undoubtedly a great honor.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 12}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5( - 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
6768.0Banished Knight's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/banished_knights_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0A finely-crafted greatsword intricately engraved with an ancient motif. G iven to knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes. Most of these knights were sent to the fringes, where they were forced to start anew with only despair for company.0{'Str': 17}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5 ( - 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
6869.0Forked Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/forked_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0Oddly shaped greatsword wielded by imps. The gently undulating forked blade is known as an "imp's tongue" and causes blood loss.0{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 16}Standard/PierceGreatswords(55) HemorrhageStamp (Upward Cut)5 (-8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
6970.0Flambergehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/flamberge_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Greatsword featuring a flame-like undulation.Shreds enemy flesh, inducing blood loss. Scarlet rot is kept at bay with flame at Redmane Castle, and as such, this beloved sword of Jerren's eventually became the symbol of the castle itself.0{'Str': 15, 'Dex': 14}Standard/PierceGreatswords(55) HemorrhageStamp (Upward Cut)5 ( - 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
7071.0Gargoyle's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/gargoyles_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.5Bronze greatsword wielded by Valiant Gargoyles. Just like the wielder, the missing parts have been mended with corpse wax; a patchwork of champions.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 10}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsVacuum Slice12nannannannannannannannannannannan
7172.0Gargoyle's Blackbladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/gargoyles_blackblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.5Bronze greatsword wielded by Valiant Gargoyles mended with blackened corpse wax. Deals holy damage. Such is the mark of those who serve Maliketh, the Black Blade.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 10, 'Fai': 22}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsCorpse Wax Cutter16nannannannannannannannannannannan
7273.0Inseparable Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/inseparable_sword_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Sword forged by compounding silver and gold. A sacred weapon to hunt Those Who Live in Death. Deals holy damage. The inseparable twins found solace in the Golden Order, the only institution not to revile them as accursed beings.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 18, 'Fai': 20}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsSacred Blade19 (- -)nannannannannannannannannannannan
7475.0Sword of Miloshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sword_of_milos_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Sinister greatsword fashioned from a giant's backbone. Metes out wounds like a lopsided saw-blade, and restores some FP upon defeating an enemy. Milos was undersized for a giant, and was viewed as sullied and terribly grotesque.0{'Str': 15, 'Dex': 19}Standard/PierceGreatswords(55) HemorrhageShriek of Milos30nannannannannannannannannannannan
7576.0Marais Executioner's Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/marais_executioners_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.5Storied sword of House Marais, the family of executioners who presided over the Shaded Castle. One of the legendary armaments. Elemer of the Briar, the Bell Bearing Hunter, snatched the sword from the site of his looming execution, and furnished it with battle skills from his home of Eochaid..0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 14, 'Arc': 23}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsEochaid's Dancing Blade15nannannannannannannannannannannan
7677.0Ordovis's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/ordoviss_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.0Greatsword of Ordovis, one of the two honored as foremostamong the Crucible Knights. This sword is imbued with an ancient holy essence.Its red tint exemplifies the nature of primordial gold, said to beclose in nature to life itself.0{'Str': 25, 'Dex': 13, 'Fai': 15}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsOrdovis's Vortex15nannannannannannannannannannannan
7778.0Alabaster Lord's Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/alabaster_lords_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0Greatsword forged from a blue-white meteoric ore.The blade conceals gravity-manipulating magic. A weapon unique to the Alabaster Lords, a race of ancients withskin of stone who were said to have risen to life when a meteorstruck long ago.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 12, 'Int': 18}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsAlabaster Lords' Pull15nannannannannannannannannannannan
7879.0Death's Pokerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/deaths_poker_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Barbed rod carried by Deathbirds. The birds are graveyard fire keepers; it is said they rake out the ashen remains of the dead from their kilns.0{'Str': 15, 'Dex': 17, 'Int': 11}Standard/PierceGreatswords(65) FrostbiteGhostflame Ignition15nannannannannannannannannannannan
7980.0Helphen's Steeplehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/helphens_steeple.png11.5Greatsword patterned after the black steeple of the Helphen, the lampwood which guides the dead of the spirit world. The lamplight is similar to grace in appearance, only it is said that it can only be seen by those who met their death in battle.0{'Str': 19, 'Dex': 10, 'Int': 22}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsRuinous Ghostflame20nannannannannannannannannannannan
8081.0Blasphemous Bladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/blasphemous_blade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png13.5Sacred sword of Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy.Remains of the countless heroes he has devoured writhe upon the surface of this blade. Now they share the same blood, bound together as family.Some HP is restored upon defeating an enemy.0{'Str': 22, 'Dex': 15, 'Fai': 21}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsTaker's Flames30nannannannannannannannannannannan
8182.0Golden Order Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/golden_order_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Greatsword made of light, modeled after the Elden Ring itself. Forged by King Consort Radagon to proudly symbolize the tenets of the Golden Order. One of the legendary armaments. Telltale signs betray that this was once the greatsword bequeathed to him by his first wife, Rennala.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 21, 'Fai': 28}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsEstablish Order20nannannannannannannannannannannan
8283.0Dark Moon Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/dark_moon_greatsword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0A Moon Greatsword, bestowed by a Carian queen upon herspouse to honor long-standing tradition.One of the legendary armaments. Ranni's sigil is a full moon, cold and leaden, and this sword is but a beam of its light.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 11, 'Int': 38}Standard/PierceGreatswords(55) FrostbiteMoonlight Greatsword32nannannannannannannannannannannan
8384.0Sacred Relic Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sacred_relic_sword.png11.0Sword wrought from the remains of a god who should have lived a life eternal. Thoughts on what the weapon portends are many and varied. Some consider it the mark of a great sin, or a sign of great devastation. Some think of it as the end of an age, while others; the beginning.0{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 24, 'Fai': 22}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsWave of Gold42nannannannannannannannannannannan
8485.0Lizard Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/lizard_greatsword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png9.5A peculiar weapon carried by imps. Greatsword smithed in the shape of a fork-tongued lizard. Strong attacks allow the wielder to fire the forked tongue from the blade.1{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 14}Strike/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5nannannannannannannannannannannan
8586.0Greatsword of Damnationhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greatsword_of_damnation_greatsword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0Golden greatsword that once pierced the body of Midra, master of the manse. Used by the hornsent in the execution of a damnation like no other. The barbs that pierce the victim from within wind gently around the blade.1{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 15, 'Fai': 20}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsGolden Crux21nannannannannannannannannannannan
8687.0Greatsword of Solitudehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greatsword_of_solitude_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png15.5Steel greatsword wielded by the Knights of the Solitary Gaol. Through a secret rite, they relinquished their hearts for heightened battle prowess. Next, they engraved their blades with two crests: one depicting the light of birth, and the other, solitude.1{'Str': 27, 'Dex': 13}Standard/PierceGreatswordsNo passive effectsSolitary Moon Slash12nannannannannannannannannannannan
8788.0Zweihanderhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/zweihander_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png15.5Just about the largest sword a mere man is capable of swinging. True to its name, it is designed to be wielded in both hands, but those of merely ordinary strength will still struggle to do so.The long blade is heavy and sharp, sending foes reeling back with its broader strokes, while also capable of a devastating piercing thrust.0{'Str': 19, 'Dex': 11}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5( -8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
8889.0Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greatsword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png23.0A coarse iron lump of an ultra greatsword. Mows down foes by utilizing its incredible weight. Though handling it likely requires the wielder to have surpassed the realm of the merely human, it is precisely for this reason the weapon is used to slaughter even inhuman foes.0{'Str': 31, 'Dex': 12}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5 ( - 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
8990.0Watchdog's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/watchdogs_greatsword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png22.0Stone greatsword wielded by Erdtree Burial Watchdogs. Though decorated with the watchman's eye, the pupil was taken by graverobbers and is now hollow, leaving this sword a mere lump of stone.0{'Str': 30, 'Dex': 10}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5 ( - 8)nannannannannannannannannannannan
9091.0Troll's Golden Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/trolls_golden_sword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png19.0Sword given to the lesser giants who fought for the Erdtree during the War against the Giants long ago. Though the giants have lost their minds, they stay inseparable from their swords that are in sad shapesuch that the gold plating had flaked away.0{'Str': 29, 'Dex': 10}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsTroll's Roar22nannannannannannannannannannannan
9192.0Troll Knight's Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/troll_knights_sword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png18.0Greatsword embedded with a blue glintstone.Weapon of the trolls in service to the royal family of Caria. Called into service when the Queen invoked an oath they swore, the trolls are treated as true knights of Caria, and fight arm in arm with their human comrades.0{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 14, 'Int': 18}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsTroll's Roar22nannannannannannannannannannannan
9293.0Royal Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/royal_greatsword__colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png20.0Greatsword decorated in royal Carian Style. Favored weapon of Blaidd the Half-Wolf. In defiance of the fate he was born to, Blaidd swore to serve no master but Ranni. As proof. the sword was imbued with a cold magic at the moment oath was sworn.0{'Str': 26, 'Dex': 18, 'Int': 22}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsWolf s Assault20nannannannannannannannannannannan
9394.0Grafted Blade Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/grafted_blade_greatsword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png21.0The storied sword of Castle Morne. A revenger's weapon, it is burdened with oceans of anger and regret. One of the legendary armaments. A lone surviving champion from a country now vanished was so determined to continue fighting that he claimed the swords of an entire clan of warriors.0{'Str': 40, 'Dex': 14}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsOath of Vengeance20nannannannannannannannannannannan
9495.0Ruins Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/ruins_greatsword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png23.0Originally rubble from a ruin which fell from the sky, this surviving fragment was honed into a weapon.One of the legendary armaments. The ruin it came from crumbled when struck by a meteorite, as such this weapon harbors its destructive power.0{'Str': 50, 'Int': 16}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsWave of Destruction25( - 25)nannannannannannannannannannannan
9596.0Starscourge Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/starscourge-greatsword-weapon-elden-ring-wiki-guide.png20.0Curved greatswords of black steel wielded by General Radahn. A pair of weapons decorated with a lion mane motif. Radahn earned considerable renown as the Starscourge in his youth, and it is said that it was during this time he engraved the gravity crest upon these blades.0{'Str': 38, 'Dex': 12, 'Int': 15}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsStarcaller Cry20 (-20)nannannannannannannannannannannan
9697.0Godslayer's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/godslayers_greatsword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png17.5Sacred sword of the Gloam-Eyed Queen who controlled the Godskin Apostles before her defeat at the hands of Maliketh. 9798.0Maliketh's Black Bladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/malikeths_black_blade_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png22.0Maliketh's black blade which once harbored the power of theRune of Death. A sad shadow of its former glory. After a fragment of Death was stolen on that fateful night,Maliketh bound the blade within his own flesh, such that nonemight ever rob Death again.0{'Str': 34, 'Dex': 12, 'Fai': 20}StandardColossal SwordsNo passive effectsDestined Death40nannannannannannannannannannannan
9899.0Ancient Meteoric Ore Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/ancient_meteoric_ore_greatsword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png22.0One of the treasures of the ruined forges. Greatsword of ancient meteoric ore, ending in a sharp point. Fashioned from an excavated shard of an arrowhead that once was a part of the old gods' arsenal. A capable piercing weapon that excels at thrusting attacks.1{'Str': 35, 'Dex': 10, 'Arc': 19}Standard/PierceColossal SwordsNo passive effectsWhite Light Charge10nannannannannannannannannannannan
99100.0Fire Knight's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/fire_knights_greatsword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png16.0Greatsword of the Fire Knights. A long, slender blade that undulates like fire. Blessed with a flame incantation. Further flame imbuement will amplify the effect and greatly increase the armament's power.1{'Str': 22, 'Dex': 18, 'Fai': 12}Standard/PierceColossal SwordsNo passive effectsStamp (Upward Cut)5nannannannannannannannannannannan
100101.0Greatsword of Radahn (Light)http://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greatsword_of_radahn_(light)_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png20.0Greatswords of black steel wielded by Radahn in his youth. A pair of weapons decorated with a lion mane motif. These were in his possession immediately before his triumph over the starsthe swords of a lord who does not rely on physical strength and gravity alone.1{'Str': 32, 'Dex': 24, 'Int': 15}Standard/PierceColossal SwordsNo passive effectsLightspeed Slash26nannannannannannannannannannannan
101102.0Greatsword of Radahn (Lord)http://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greatsword_of_radahn_(lord)_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png19.0Greatswords of black steel wielded by Radahn in his youth. 102103.0Moonrithyll's Knight Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/troll_knights_sword_colossal_swords_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png18.0Greatsword embedded with a blue glintstone. The favored weapon of the steadfast Carian knight, Moonrithyll. Moonrithyll was the Twin Moon Knight's chamberlain. She was also a friend to the trolls, who served the royal family, and proudly wielded their weapons as she fought arm in arm with her gargantuan comrades.1{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 14, 'Int': 18}Standard/PierceColossal SwordsNo passive effectsTremendous Phalanx16nannannannannannannannannannannan
104105.0Rapierhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/rapier_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.5A piercing sword with a sharp, slender blade.In addition to this weapon's thrusting attack prowess, it also boasts the ability to be used while guarding with a shield. As one of the smaller piercing swords, quick successive thrust area easy to perform, but without a cutting edge, it cannot be used for slashing attacks.0{'Str': 7, 'Dex': 12}Standard/PierceThrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust14nannannannannannannannannannannan
105106.0Estochttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/estoc_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Large piercing sword with a long, hard bladed edge which is also capable of slashing attacks. For its large size, it can be wielded rather nimbly, leaving little opportunity for the enemy to counter.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 13}Standard/PierceThrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust14nannannannannannannannannannannan
106107.0Noble's Estochttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/nobles_estoc_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5Estoc carried by wandering nobles. Made to be easy to wield. Daubed with gold from tip to tail, it should fetch a good sum.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 10}Standard/PierceThrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
107108.0Cleanrot Knight's Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/cleanrot_knight's_sword_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0Piercing sword of the Cleanrot Knights who fought alongside Malenia, Blade of Miquella. Mainly used as a backup weapon, in combination with a long- handled armament.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 13}Standard/PierceThrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
108109.0Rogier's Rapierhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/rogiers_rapier_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5Piercing sword of superior quality, featuring intricate ornamentation. Signature weapon of the sorcerer Rogier. High dexterity is required to wield the blade to its full potential, but mastery is a sight to behold, characterized by a flowing style which excels in successive attacks.0{'Str': 8, 'Dex': 17}Standard/PierceThrusting SwordsNo passive effectsRepeating Thrust10nannannannannannannannannannannan
109110.0Antspur Rapierhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/antspur_rapier_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.0Spur of a giant ant which has been fashioned into a rapier. The blade drips with scarlet rot. Scarlet rot is an old legend, of which Maleigh Marais of the Shaded Castle was a private believer. And indeed, he eventually found his own personal goddess.0{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 20}Standard/PierceThrusting Swords(55) Scarlet RotImpaling Thrust14nannannannannannannannannannannan
110111.0Frozen Needlehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/frozen_needle_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.5A razor-thin piercing blade of ice.Forged by Ijii, the carian Royal Blackmith. Can inflict frost upon enemies, and launch its blade with a strong attack. The blade immediately regenerates.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 18}Standard/PierceThrusting Swords(60) FrostbiteImpaling Thrust14nannannannannannannannannannannan
111112.0Carian Sorcery Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/carian_sorcery_sword_thrusting_sword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png2.5One of the Carian royal family's glintstone swords. A smaller piercing sword with a slender blade. Formerly used by the Carian knights of old before the full refinement of their sorcerous combat techniques, this armament functions as a catalyst for glintstone sorcery when executing strong attacks.1{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 19, 'Int': 16}Standard/PierceThrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
112113.0Great Epeehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/great_epee_heavy_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.5Large rapier featuring a thin, sharp blade. Commonly used in life or death duels.Like its smaller counterparts, its made for thrusting attacks, and can be used while guarding with a shield.0{'Str': 15, 'Dex': 16}Standard/PierceHeavy Thrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
113114.0Godskin Stitcherhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/godskin_stitcher_heavy_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png7.0Elegant piercing sword with a celadon colored blade wielded by Godskin Nobles. The nobles possess skill with the sword unmatched by any lowborn. Despite its size, successive attacks from this weapon are swifter than the eye can follow.0{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 17}Standard/PierceHeavy Thrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
114115.0Bloody Helicehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bloody_helice_heavy_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0Ominous piercing sword with a winding blade. Carried by the noble servants of the Lord of Blood. Designed to bore into flesh, causing severe blood loss at the wound. The extracted blood trickles gracefully down the length of the blade.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 19, 'Arc': 17}Standard/PierceHeavy Thrusting Swords(55) HemorrhageDynast's Finesse5 ( -7)nannannannannannannannannannannan
115116.0Dragon King's Cragbladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/dragon_kings_cragblade_heavy_thrusting_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0Piercing Gravel Stone sword containing primeval lightning. A portion of the Dragonlord's power, gained from a remembrance. This weapon commands great power over the paltry, mortal dragons of today.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 37}Standard/PierceHeavy Thrusting SwordsNo passive effectsThundercloud Form28nannannannannannannannannannannan
116117.0Queelign's Greatswordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/queeligns_greatsword_heavy_thrusting_sword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png7.5Greatsword of the Fire Knight Queelign. A weighty piercing sword with a blade that undulates like fire. Queelign devoted his all to the crusade, and remade his greatsword in the shape of the lance wielded by his liegeaspiring to become a second Impaler.1{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 18, 'Fai': 12}Standard/PierceHeavy Thrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
117118.0Sword Lancehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sword_lance_heavy_thrusting_sword_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0Weapon once wielded by Commander Gaius. A combination of greatsword and lance. Weighty and capable of being brought to bear with great force, this armament shows its true potential when used astride a mount. Gaius himself was never without his boar.1{'Str': 21, 'Dex': 11}Standard/PierceHeavy Thrusting SwordsNo passive effectsImpaling Thrust9nannannannannannannannannannannan
118119.0Scimitarhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/scimitar_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.0A curved sword with a single-sided blade. Delivers slashing attacks with its sharp blade, but is ineffective against armor and hides covered in tough scales. Smaller in size than most curved swords, it carves into enemies with quick, sharp cuts.0{'Str': 7, 'Dex': 13}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 (- 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
119120.0Falchionhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/falchion_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5A curved sword with a single-sided blade. Wielders of the weapon employ a unique style that enhances attacks with spinning motions.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 13}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( -12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
120121.0Shamshirhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/shamshir_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5Curved sword with a thin blade of ample length. Light of weight despite its larger size, its slicing attacks come in rapid succession. A devastatingly powerful weapon in the hands of a skilled swordsman.0{'Str': 7, 'Dex': 13}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
121122.0Grossmesserhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/grossmesser_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Curved sword boasting a large blade. This weapon is designed to make use of its weight in combination with its sharp cutting edge. A thoroughly reliable choice of weapon for those who can master its particulars.0{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 12}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
122123.0Bandit's Curved Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bandits_curved_sword_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0Curved sword with a light, crescent-shaped blade. Used by the bandits who ran rampant in the wake of the Shattering. 123124.0Shotelhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/shotel_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.0Curved sword with a severely hooked blade. Made to hunt down humans, this weapon requires high dexterity to wield efficiently.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 19}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 (-12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
124125.0Scavenger's Curved Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/scavengers_curved_sword_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5Unique curved sword, notched like shark's teeth. 125126.0Mantis Bladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/mantis_blade_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0A curved sword with a blade at both ends wielded by the Cemetery Shades, the insect-ridden grave keepers. 126127.0Beastman's Curved Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/beastmans_curved_sword_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0Curved sword forged of dull iron wielded by the beastmen of Farum Azula. The blade is heavy but well-balanced. Comparatively easy to wield for the damage it delivers.0{'Str': 13, 'Dex': 11}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
127128.0Flowing Curved Swordhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/flowing_curved_sword_curved_sword_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5Legends speak of a master of the sword garbed in blue, and his curved blade that was patterned after flowing water. Additional input allows for a follow-up attack.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 17}SlashCurved SwordsNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan

Gelmir. 157158.0Sword of Nighthttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sword_of_night_katana_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png6.5Sword of an all-consuming, bottomless black that devours even the light that shines upon it. Wielded by Jolan, Swordhand of Night. The blade of the sword is only semicorporeal, and thus cannot be guarded against completely.1{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 20}Slash/PierceKatanas(45) HemorrhageWitching Hour Slash21nannannannannannannannannannannan
158159.0Twinbladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/twinblade_twinblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png7.0A sword with blades on both sides of the hilt.This weapon is difficult to wield, and requires high dexterity. Can be wielded with a single hand, but its true strength is enabled with dual-handed spinning attacks.0{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 18}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
159160.0Twinned Knight Swordshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/twinned_knight_swords_twinblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0An attractive twinblade of fine make that prizes the chivalric way. Can be wielded with a single hand, but its true strength is unlocked with dual-handed spinning attacks.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 18}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
160161.0Godskin Peelerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/godskin_peeler_twinblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0Unique twinblade wielded by Godskin Apostles characterized by its disturbing design. One end features a sickle for slicing attacks while the other boasts a winding spike for boring into flesh. Much skill is required to wield this weapon due to its asymmetric nature.0{'Str': 17, 'Dex': 22}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsBlack Flame Tornado30nannannannannannannannannannannan
161162.0Gargoyle's Twinbladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/gargoyles_twinblade_twinblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Bronze twinblade wielded by Valiant Gargoyles. Just like the wielder, the missing parts have been mended with corpse wax; a patchwork of champions.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 15}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6( -12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
162163.0Gargoyle's Black Bladeshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/gargoyles_black_blades_twinblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Bronze twinblade wielded by Valiant Gargoyles, mended with blackened corpse wax. Deals holy damage. Such is the mark of those who serve Maliketh, the Black Blade.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 15, 'Fai': 22}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
163164.0Eleonora's Polebladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/eleonoras_poleblade_twinblade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0Twinned naginata forged in the Land of Reeds. Chosen weapon of Eleonora, Violet Bloody Finger. Her mastery of the sword was such that her onslaught was likened to a whirlwind, but now her legacy is stained by accursed blood.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 21, 'Arc': 19}Standard/PierceTwinblades(55) HemorrhageBloodblade Dance11 ( - 6)nannannannannannannannannannannan
164165.0Black Steel Twinbladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/black_steel_twinblade_twinblade_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0Weapon of the Black Knights, servants of Messmer the Impaler. Twinblade of black steel with decorative embellishments in gold. Blessed with an Erdtree incantation. Further holy imbuement will amplify the effect and greatly increase the armament's power.1{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 15, 'Fai': 13}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsSpinning Slash6nannannannannannannannannannannan
165166.0Euporiahttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/euporia_twinblade_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Twinblade symbolizing abundance. The secret treasure of the tower. Though the blades, fashioned from golden shoots, are largely wilted and darkened, their luster can be restored by dealing damage to foes. However, damage dealt to Those Who Live in Death will have no such effect.1{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 16, 'Fai': 24}Standard/PierceTwinbladesNo passive effectsEuporia Vortex28nannannannannannannannannannannan
166167.0Hand Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/hand_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5Commonly known as a hatchet, this smaller variety of axe is an everyday work tool. Despite its short reach, it is easy to handle, exceling in successive attacks.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 8}StandardAxesNo passive effectsQuickstep3nannannannannannannannannannannan
167168.0Forked Hatchethttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/forked_hatchet_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.5Peculiar hatchet wielded by imps. The gently undulating forked blade is known as an "imp's tongue" and causes blood loss.0{'Str': 9, 'Dex': 14}StandardAxes(50) HemorrhageQuickstep3nannannannannannannannannannannan
168169.0Battle Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/battle_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5A thick, heavy blade attached to a handle. A versatile, standard weaponCapable of powerful attacks thanks to the weighty blade, these weapons are also regarded for their powerful charge attacks.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 8}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes1 (6 10)nannannannannannannannannannannan
169170.0Warped Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/warped_axe_weapons_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png7.5Oversized double-headed axe with a bizarre, almost melted appearance. Brute strength is the only thing required to wield this weapon, which appears to have taken quite the beating itself.0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 8}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
170171.0Jawbone Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/jawbone_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Axe made from a herbivore's skull. Weapon of the ancestral followers who disdain metal. This axe is more of a bludgeon; it forgoes a bladed edge, instead using the beast's molar teeth to buffet foes, dealing strike damage.0{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 8}StrikeAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes1 (6 10)nannannannannannannannannannannan
171172.0Iron Cleaverhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/iron_cleaver_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0Fairly large iron cleaver, commonly used by the maltreated Misbegotten. Steeped in resentment, these weapons are swung wildly and relentlessly, often after rushing up to foes.0{'Str': 15, 'Dex': 7}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes1 (6 10)nannannannannannannannannannannan
172173.0Highland Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/highland_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Single-sided axe used by the warriors of the highlands. Brave combatants begin battle by crying out their names. Roars are enhanced by this weapon.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 9}StandardAxesRoar BuffWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
173174.0Celebrant's Cleaverhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/celebrants-cleaver_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Cleaver decorated with many-colored fabrics. Ceremonial tool used by dancers during the festivities of Dominula, Windmill Village. Crafted from human bone. Grants trace amounts of runes upon landing attacks.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 8}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes2nannannannannannannannannannannan
174175.0Sacrificial Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/sacrificial_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Hatchet used in ancient sacrificial rite. A Deathbird is depicted as a malevolent deity. The power of the rite yet lingers. A small amount of FP is restored upon slaying a foe.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 10}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes2 (10 15)nannannannannannannannannannannan
175176.0Icerind Hatchethttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/icerind_hatchet_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.0A hatchet with a frost-coated blade. One of several gifts given by Castle Sol in the distant north. Known as "freezing fog," the blade is thought to be a dragon's scale. Inflicts a powerful frost effect.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 16}StandardAxes(65) FrostbiteHoarfrost Stomp10nannannannannannannannannannannan
176177.0Ripple Bladehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/ripple_blade_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Unique weapon wielded by young Albinaurics, this sword is modelled after the ripples that are thought to be the origin of their species. Its attack power is greatly enhanced with one's arcane attribute.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 11, 'Arc': 20}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes2 (10 15)nannannannannannannannannannannan
177178.0Stormhawk Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/stormhawk_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Battle axe designed to resemble a hawk, with its wings comprising the blade. Signature weapon of warriors who strive to remain one with the storm, despite being so far from their place of birth. Their hearts are proud, and thereby easily undone.0{'Str': 19, 'Dex': 15}StandardAxesNo passive effectsThunderstorm19 ( - 12)nannannannannannannannannannannan
178179.0Rosus' Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/rosus_axe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Usher of Death, Rosus, who shows the path to the catacombs throughout the Lands Between, is depicted on this ritual axe. The dead easily lose their way, and have always been in sore need of a guiding hand.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 10, 'Int': 18}StandardAxesNo passive effectsRosus's Summons15nannannannannannannannannannannan
179180.0Forked-Tongue Hatchethttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/forked-tongue_hatchet_unique_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0A peculiar weapon wielded by imps. Hatchet smithed in the shape of a fork-tongued dragon. The gently undulating forked blade is known as an "imp's tongue" and causes blood loss.1{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 13}Standar/PierceAxes(50) HemorrhageDragonform Flame14nannannannannannannannannannannan
180181.0Smithscript Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/smithscript_axe_axes_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0Axe engraved with a smithscript. A throwing weapon created through the smithing arts. Execute a strong attack or jumping strong attack to throw the axe.1{'Str': 13, 'Dex': 10, 'Int': 11, 'Fai': 11}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWild Strikes2nannannannannannannannannannannan
181182.0Messmer Soldier's Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/messmer_soldiers_axe_axes_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Weapon used by the soldiers who serve Messmer the Impaler. A steel axe, worn from use. A favorite of those who lost themselves utterly in their lord's war. Capable of performing powerful charge attacks.1{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 10}StandardAxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
182183.0Death Knight's Twin Axeshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/death_knights_twin_axes_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Golden war axes of the Death Knight. A pair of weapons made to be wielded in both hands. Crackles with lightning, the power of the capital's ancient dragon cult. The knight, once the personal guard of Godwyn, was also the protector of the Prince of Death's cadaver surrogate.1{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 12, 'Fai': 16}StandardAxesNo passive effectsBlinkbolt: Twinaxe8nannannannannannannannannannannan
183184.0Greataxehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greataxe_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png13.0Greataxe akin to a huge lump of iron. Recognized as the weapon of choice of badland chieftains.Too heavy to be wielded by those of merely ordinary strength, blows from this axe will flatten enemies, and the force of its charged attack is a sight to behold.0{'Str': 30, 'Dex': 8}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
184185.0Crescent Moon Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/crescent_moon_axe_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.5Greataxe with a large, arcing blade and long handle. The "Crescent Moon" of its name is said to have been spurred by nostalgia. Specializes in sweeping through foes by making use of its long handle.0{'Str': 25, 'Dex': 15}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
185186.0Longhaft Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/longhaft_axe_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.5Large iron axe with a long handle. Weapon of the maltreated Misbegotten. Making use of the force generated by its length, this weapon's resentment-charged blows are difficult to stop.0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 8}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
186187.0Executioner's Greataxehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/executioners_greataxe_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png15.0Bulky cleaver welded to a long haft. Said to have been used to dispatch the remnants of defeated armies, felling them like timber. Perhaps that is the reason it excels at critical hits.0{'Str': 34, 'Dex': 8}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
187188.0Great Omenkiller Cleaverhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/great_omenkiller_cleaver_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.0The blade of this huge, loathsome cleaver comprises a row of amputated Omen horns. Weapon of slaughter wielded by Omen killers. The hideous horns cause blood loss, adding vibrant colors to the ongoing mayhem.0{'Str': 23, 'Dex': 12}StandardGreataxes(55) HemorrhageWild Strike2 (per swing)nannannannannannannannannannannan
188189.0Rusted Anchorhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/rusted_anchor_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.5A rusty anchor wielded as a weapon. Each of its four flukes is thick and sharp, enabling piercing attacks. While the Tarnished left the Lands Between with their Lord, one boat alone was said to have been left behind.0{'Str': 26, 'Dex': 9}PierceGreataxesNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
189190.0Butchering Knifehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/butchering_knife_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Huge carving knife made to cleanly butcher the human body.Signature weapon of the Ogress Anastasia, known to have eatencountless Tarnished while disguised as a Finger Maiden. Restores a very small amount of HP when it squarely strikes an enemy.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 20}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
190191.0Gargoyle's Great Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/gargoyles_great_axe_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Bronze greataxe wielded by Valiant Gargoyles. Just like the wielder, the missing parts have been mended with corpse wax; a patchwork of champions.0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 8}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
191192.0Gargoyle's Black Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/gargoyles_black_axe_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Bronze greataxe wielded by Valiant Gargoyles, mended with blackened corpse wax. Deals holy damage. Such is the mark of those who serve Maliketh, the Black Blade.0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 8, 'Fai': 22}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsWar Cry16nannannannannannannannannannannan
192193.0Winged Greathornhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/winged_greathorn_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.0Distinctive horn suffused with the power of ancestral spirits. This large, wing-shaped specimen is wielded as a weapon of spirit worship.In the ancestral spirit-worshipping faith, these are considered envoys' wings, made to reap the lives of beings which experience no sprouting.0{'Str': 30, 'Dex': 20}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsSoul Stifler12nannannannannannannannannannannan
193194.0Axe of Godrickhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/axe_of_godrick_greataxe_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.0Greataxe wielded by Godrick the Grafted. This golden battleaxe is emblazoned with the figure of a beast, representing the strength of Godfrey, First Elden Lord and patriarch of the golden lineage. "I command thee kneel! For I am the lord of all that is golden!"0{'Str': 34, 'Dex': 22}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsI Command Thee, Kneel!15nannannannannannannannannannannan
194195.0Putrescence Cleaverhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/putrescence_cleaver_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png13.5Weapon wielded by the Putrescent Knight. A great cleaver of hardened putrescence affixed to an arch fashioned from human bones. Gains power from the wielder's arcane attribute.1{'Str': 28, 'Dex': 10, 'Arc': 15}SlashGreataxes(55) FrostbiteSpinning Guillotine12nannannannannannannannannannannan
195196.0Death Knight's Longhaft Axehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/death_knights_longhaft_axe_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png11.5A long-hafted golden axe wielded by the Death Knight. Bears a representation of a decayed golden ring. Crackles with lightning, the power of the capital's ancient dragon cult. The knight, once the personal guard of Godwyn, was also the protector of the Prince of Death's cadaver surrogate.1{'Str': 23, 'Dex': 10, 'Fai': 18}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsBlinkbolt: Long-hafted Axe8nannannannannannannannannannannan
196197.0Bonny Butchering Knifehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bonny_butchering_knife_greataxe_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Weapon of the greater potentates of Bonny Village. An outsize butcher's cleaver used to dismember human bodies in the making of the great jars stored in the gaols. Restores a very small amount of HP when it squarely strikes an enemy.1{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 20}StandardGreataxesNo passive effectsHone Blade16nannannannannannannannannannannan
197198.0Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/club_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png3.5A thick, solid lump of wood.Wielding this striking weapon requires no skill. A simple, primitive weapon that requires only brute strength and persistence to hammer your foe into the ground.0{'Str': 10}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
198199.0Curved Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/curved_club_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0Hard, curved club, shaped like a crescent moon. Wielded instead of a curved sword by Albinaurics lacking dexterity. The only point of similarity between the two is the shape.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 7}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
199200.0Spiked Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/spiked_club_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0A club with beast fangs wrapped around the striking end. The uneven spread of fangs causes blood loss. Given only to the toughest of demi-humans, this weapon is a badge of honor. Or so the demi-humans believe.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 7}StrikeHammers(50) HemorrhageBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
200201.0Stone Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/stone_club_clubs_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png7.0Signature weapon of battlemages, hewn from hard glintstone. This club is much heavier than its size suggests, but has no magic power. So that they not lose sight of the true essence of combat, battlemages prefer to feel the impact of their blows, fighting their opponents hand to hand.0{'Str': 16, 'Dex': 8}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsKick0nannannannannannannannannannannan
201202.0Macehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/mace_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5An iron bludgeon with sharp protrusions. Striking attacks have few drawbacks, potent as they are at breaking through guards and stances, Most suited to head on slugging matches.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 7}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsKick0nannannannannannannannannannannan
202203.0Morning Starhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/morning_star_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0Battle hammer comprised of a globe attached to a handle. Though a bludgeon dealing strike damage, the appellative star is covered in spikes which cause blood loss. Ironic given its graceful name, this weapon often reeks of blood.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 8}StrikeHammers(50) HemorrhageKick0nannannannannannannannannannannan
203204.0Warpickhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/warpick_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.5Battle hammer with a hard protruding beak. A pickaxe designed for combat, in essence. A simple but highly effective weapon, the weighty piercing attack casually rips through metal armor.0{'Str': 11, 'Dex': 9}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsKick0nannannannannannannannannannannan
204205.0Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/hammer_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.5Hammer comprised of a large stone affixed to a metal handle.Originally a blacksmith's tool. The art of smithing is said to have originated among the giants.This weapon's striking attacks boast ample weight behind them.0{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 7}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsKick0nannannannannannannannannannannan
205206.0Monk's Flamemacehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/monks_flamemace_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0Battle hammer made to resemble flickering flames. Signature weapon of the Fire Monks, who hail from the Mountaintops of the Giants. The unique strong attack rouses fiery combative spirit.0{'Str': 13, 'Dex': 13}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsKick0nannannannannannannannannannannan
206207.0Varre's Bouquethttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/varres_bouquet_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png2.0A steel mace resembling a charming bouquet of roses. Each petal has a sharpened edge, leaving the roses perpetually colored with blood. This weapon reflects White Mask Varre's manner of speech rather well, enticing in its splendor, but full of deadly consequence.0{'Str': 8, 'Dex': 16, 'Arc': 24}StrikeHammersCauses blood loss buildup (65)Blood Tax14nannannannannannannannannannannan
207208.0Envoy's Hornhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/envoys_horn_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.0Golden horn of the Oracle Envoys. Profoundly weighty, its blows are sure to be felt. Originally an instrument, but one that cannot be sounded by a mere human. Or perhaps it is too early to sound the call.0{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 12, 'Fai': 16}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsOracular Bubble- 6nannannannannannannannannannannan
208209.0Nox Flowing Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/nox_flowing_hammer_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.5Mace shaped like a suspended metal droplet wielded by monks of the Eternal City. Forged from liquid metal from a Silver Tear, it is thoroughly tempered until hardened.0{'Str': 17, 'Dex': 7}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsFlowing Form16nannannannannannannannannannannan
209210.0Ringed Fingerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/ringed_finger_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Bludgeon made of an enormous finger sheathed in several heavy rings. Thought to have been cut from an ancestor of the Fingercreeper. Some life yet remains in this legacy of an ancient act of blasphemy, as evidenced by the barely perceptible warmth it still exudes.0{'Str': 15, 'Dex': 9}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsClaw Flick14nannannannannannannannannannannan
210211.0Scepter of the All-Knowinghttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/scepter_of_the_all-knowing_hammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png4.5Scepter in the form of a hand grasping a pearl. Signature weaponof Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing. The pearl stands for theworld, the heavens, and an eye, representing the many forms ofknowledge, never fully attainable. Even knowing that, the All-Knowing's hand grasps for it.0{'Str': 12, 'Dex': 18, 'Int': 21}Strike/PierceHammersNo passive effectsKnowledge Above All35nannannannannannannannannannannan
211212.0Marika's Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/marikas_hammer_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0Stone hammer made in the lands of the Numen, outside the Lands Between. The tool with which Queen Marika shattered the Elden Ring and Radagon attempted to repair it. The hammer partially broke upon shattering the Ring, becoming splintered with rune fragments.0{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 12, 'Fai': 19}StrikeHammersNo passive effectsGold Breaker26nannannannannannannannannannannan
212213.0Flowerstone Gavelhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/flowerstone_gavel_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0Weapon of the Dragon Communion priestess. A dragonstone hammer resembling an ancient flower. The four petals and the flower's core are all sharp, ending in points, and are capable of dealing heavy damage to dragons.1{'Str': 14, 'Dex': 8, 'Arc': 15}PierceHammersNo passive effectsFlower Dragonbolt18nannannannannannannannannannannan
213214.0Flailhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/flail_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.0Weapon consisting of a bludgeoning head attached to a handle by chain. The iron ball is spiked and induces blood loss. Blows from this weapon cannot be parried. The charge attack whirls the iron ball around for increased force before striking.0{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 18}StrikeFlails(50) HemorrhageSpinning Chain8 (8 10)nannannannannannannannannannannan
214215.0Nightrider Flailhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/nightrider_flail_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0A flail with two additional bludgeoning heads. Weapon of the Night's Cavalry who ride funeral steeds. The large spikes make it highly effective at inducing blood loss, but also demand higher dexterity to wield.0{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 24}StrikeFlails(50) HemorrhageSpinning Chain8 (8 10)nannannannannannannannannannannan
215216.0Chainlink Flailhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/chainlink_flail_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.0A spiked iron tube attached by a chain wielded by Mad Pumpkin Heads. Especially large for a flail, wielding it requires more strength than it does dexterity.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 12}StrikeFlails(50) HemorrhageSpinning Chain8 (8 10)nannannannannannannannannannannan
216217.0Family Headshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/family_heads_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Three bludgeoning copper heads attached to a handle by chains. Signature weapon of Necromancer Garris, the heretical sage.The heads were made to resemble those of his wife and two children.0{'Str': 8, 'Dex': 18, 'Int': 16}StrikeFlailsNo passive effectsFamilial Rancor25nannannannannannannannannannannan
217218.0Bastard's Starshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bastards_stars_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png5.5Flail which deals magic damage, having been imbued with power by the remembrance of Astel. Formed of the same many-colored star debris that comprised the form of the Naturalborn of the Void.0{'Str': 8, 'Dex': 22, 'Int': 22}StrikeFlailsNo passive effectsNebula25nannannannannannannannannannannan
218219.0Serpent Flailhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/serpent_flail_flails_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png6.0A flail with coiled snakes for bludgeoning heads. Fire roars within the snakes' bodies, inflicting horrible burns. Some of Messmer's army used these flails to burn hornsentto brand them with agony and fear.1{'Str': 10, 'Dex': 24, 'Fai': 21}StrikeFlailsNo passive effectsFlare, O Serpent8nannannannannannannannannannannan
219220.0Large Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/large_club_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5A thick, solid lump of wood. Wielding this striking weapon requires no skill. A simple, primitive weapon that requires only brute strength to swing about.0{'Str': 22}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
220221.0Curved Great Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/curved_great_club_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Large curved club of hard wood, shaped like a crescent moon. Wielded by Albinaurics lacking the dexterity demanded by a curved sword, and who prefer the added weight and destructive force.0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 10}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
221222.0Great Macehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/great_mace_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.5Huge bludgeon with sharp protrusions. An enlarged version of its smaller cousin. Maintains all the advantages of smaller bludgeons, boasting few deficiencies while being very capable at breaking enemy guards and stances. A shrewd choice for simple slugging matches.0{'Str': 28}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsEndure9nannannannannannannannannannannan
222223.0Pickaxehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/pickaxe_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Large pickaxe designed to crack thick bedrock. Tool of sturdy miners. Not originally designed as a weapon, but very adept at piercing armor. "Little wretches, do you think your armor harder than stone?"0{'Str': 22, 'Dex': 10}PierceGreat HammersNo passive effectsEndure9nannannannannannannannannannannan
223224.0Brick Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/brick_hammer_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.5Weapon made from an ordinary stone brick. Wielded by a laborer who lead a rebellion, and later become a champion himself. Even among other bludgeons of its size, this weapon is especially weighty. The strength of a giant is required to wield it.0{'Str': 31}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
225226.0Battle Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/battle_hammer_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Large iron great hammer designed for gladiatorial combat. Used by duelists who were exiled from the colosseum. Weighty enough to crush armor and its wearer alike.0{'Str': 26, 'Dex': 8}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBraggart's Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
226227.0Rotten Battle Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/rotten_battle_hammer_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Large iron great hammer designed for gladiatorial combat, now festering with scarlet rot. Weighty enough to crush armor and its wearer alike. Skill: Braggart's Roar Declare your presence with a boastful roar. Raises attack power, defense, and stamina recovery speed.0{'Str': 26, 'Dex': 8}StrikeGreat Hammers(65) Scarlet RotBraggart's Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
227228.0Celebrant's Skullhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/celebrants_skull_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png8.5Large bludgeon decorated with flowers and many-colored fabrics. Ceremonial tool used by dancers during the festivities of Dominula, Windmill Village. The striking end is a skull too large in size to be human. Grants trace amounts of runes on landing attacks.0{'Str': 18, 'Dex': 12}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
228229.0Great Starshttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/great_stars_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Huge bludgeon with three stars at the striking end. Though primarily a striking weapon, the stars' spikes cause blood loss.A blood-stained star is an ill omen, a fact not lost upon those against whom this weapon is brought to bear. Landing attacks slightly restores HP.0{'Str': 22, 'Dex': 12}StrikeGreat Hammers(55) HemorrhageEndure9nannannannannannannannannannannan
230231.0Greathorn Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/greathorn_hammer_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png10.0Huge thick horn carved with tree ring-like markings adapted into a bludgeoning weapon. Wielded by ancestral follower warriors. The ancient horn is imbued with the power of ancestral spirits. A small amount of HP is restored upon defeating an enemy.0{'Str': 22, 'Dex': 10}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBarbaric Roar16nannannannannannannannannannannan
231232.0Envoy's Long Hornhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/envoys_long_horn_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png9.5Long golden horn of the Oracle Envoys. Profoundly weighty, its blows are sure to be felt. Originally an instrument, but one that cannot be sounded by a mere human. Or perhaps it is too early to sound the call.0{'Str': 23, 'Dex': 11, 'Fai': 18}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsBubble Shower16nannannannannannannannannannannan
232233.0Cranial Vessel Candlestandhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/cranial_vessel_candlestand_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png12.5Candlestand used in worship of Birac, the most hallowed MonkPrelate of the Giants' Flame. Concerned over the lazy arrogancewith which the monks treated the Flame, Birac severed his ownhead as a lesson to the others, intending it be used as a candlestand. His faith yet smolders from within the cranial vessel.0{'Str': 26, 'Dex': 8, 'Fai': 22}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsSurge of Faith15 (-15)nannannannannannannannannannannan
233234.0Beastclaw Greathammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/beastclaw_greathammer_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0Greathammer with a striking end modelled to resemble five beastly claws. The black nails protruding from golden fur are said to represent Serosh, Lord of Beasts, who went to become King Godfrey's Regent.0{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 10, 'Fai': 18}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsRegal Beastclaw20nannannannannannannannannannannan
234235.0Devourer's Scepterhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/devourers_scepter_warhammer_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png11.5Scepter in the shape of a serpent devouring the world. Thisweapon will one day become the very symbol of the Lord of Blasphemy.One of the legendary armaments. A vision of the future briefly seen by Rykard in his final momentsbefore being devoured by the great serpent.0{'Str': 24, 'Dex': 20, 'Fai': 25}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsDevourer of Worlds35nannannannannannannannannannannan
235236.0Black Steel Greathammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/black_steel_greathammer_great_hammer_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png12.0Weapon of the Black Knights, servants of Messmer the Impaler. Greathammer of black steel with decorative embellishments in gold. Blessed with an Erdtree incantation. Further holy imbuement will amplify the effect and greatly increase the armament's power. Guard Counters cause a burst of power.1{'Str': 25, 'Dex': 11, 'Fai': 17}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsEndure9nannannannannannannannannannannan
236237.0Smithscript Greathammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/smithscript_greathammer_great_hammer_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png9.0Greathammer engraved with a smithscript. A throwing weapon created through the smithing arts. Execute a strong attack or dashing strong attack to spin and fling the greathammer.1{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 10, 'Int': 11, 'Fai': 11}StrikeGreat HammersNo passive effectsEndure9nannannannannannannannannannannan

238239.0Rotten Greataxehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/rotten_greataxe_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png20.0Greataxe designed for gladiatorial combat, now festering with scarlet rot. 240241.0Giant-Crusherhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/giant-crusher_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png26.5A hammer made from a boulder, used in the War against the Giants.One of the heftiest weapons in the entire Lands Between. After the giants were quelled, and man turned against man in violence, this weapon was all but forgotten. Man has grown feeble in comparison to his forebears.0{'Str': 60}StrikeColossal WeaponsNo passive effectsEndure9nannannannannannannannannannannan
241242.0Prelate's Inferno Crozierhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/prelates_inferno_crozier_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png23.5Colossal hammer with the appearance of roiling flames. 242243.0Great Clubhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/great_club_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png17.0An enormous club of hardwood. Wildly hammering foes with this striking weapon requires no dexterity; only brute force. While it may seem sacrilegious, this weapon is said to be a withered branch of the Erdtree. Imbued with holy power, this weapon will never snap.0{'Str': 35}StrikeColossal WeaponsNo passive effectsGolden Land16 ( - 5)nannannannannannannannannannannan
243244.0Troll's Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/trolls_hammer_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png16.0Mining tool of stonedigger trolls used to crack bedrock. Trolls are descended from the giants, and these were supposedly once used as ceremonial smithing tools. In the distant past, smithing was considered divine.0{'Str': 28, 'Dex': 8, 'Fai': 10}StrikeColossal WeaponsNo passive effectsTroll's Roar22nannannannannannannannannannannan
244245.0Dragon Greatclawhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/dragon_greatclaw_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png16.0Weapon said to have been whittled from the claw of a great, ancient dragon, wielded by grotesque Tree Sentinels who yet serve the Erdtree. 245246.0Watchdog's Staffhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/watchdog_staff_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png19.0Large stone staff embedded with glintstone. Wielded by Erdtree Burial Watchdogs who protect catacombs. The Watchdogs, battered and broken over their lengthy tenure, rule the catacombs and are even said to command the imps. Unique Skill: Sorcery of the Crozier Channel magic into the glintstone to activate an ancient sorcery, producing floating magic projectiles that chase enemies automatically.0{'Str': 34, 'Dex': 10}StrikeColossal WeaponsNo passive effectsSorcery of the Crozier15nannannannannannannannannannannan
246247.0Staff of the Avatarhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/staff_of_the_avatar_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png20.0Ceremonial staff depicting the Erdtree in its historic radiance. Wielded by the avatars who protect the Minor Erdtrees. 251252.0Axe of Godfreyhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/axe_of_godfrey_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png18.0Weapon of Godfrey, Elden Lord. It was broken in a battle fought as leader of the Tarnished during the Long March. This weapon is symbolic of Godfrey's vow to conduct himself as a lord, later becoming an emblem of the golden lineage. In the days of the past, a crown was warranted with strength.0{'Str': 42, 'Dex': 14}StandardColossal WeaponsNo passive effectsRegal Roar25nannannannannannannannannannannan
252253.0Bloodfiend's Armhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/bloodfiends_arm_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png20.0Weapon used by the bloodfiends. 253254.0Anvil Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/anvil_hammer_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png22.0One of the treasures of the ruined forges. A tremendous hammer that incorporates as its head the red-hot anvil of the forge. It is said that the anvil's shape is inspired by that of an altar, and that long ago, it was indeed used as such in rite and ritual.1{'Str': 39, 'Dex': 10, 'Int': 11, 'Fai': 20}StrikeColossal WeaponsNo passive effectsSmithing Art Spears18nannannannannannannannannannannan
254255.0Devonia's Hammerhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/devonias_hammer_unique_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png20.0Great hammer wielded by Devonia, longest-serving of the Crucible Knights. 255256.0Shadow Sunflower Blossomhttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/shadow_sunflower_blossom_colossal_weapon_elden_ring_shadow_of_the_erdtree_dlc_wiki_guide_200px.png16.0The head of a monstrous shadow sunflower manifestation of the Scadutree. 261262.0Pikehttp://eldenring.wiki.fextralife.com/file/Elden-Ring/pike_spear_weapon_elden_ring_wiki_guide_200px.png7.5A longspear featuring an especially lengthy handle. Incredibly useful even against foes on horseback, but hard to wield in cramped spaces.0{'Str': 20, 'Dex': 14}PierceSpearsNo passive effectsCharge Forth16nannannannannannannannannannannan
